title: Course Syllabus
text:
About



Organizers: SciLifeLab Training Hub
Date: 14-15 May 2025
Location: SciLifeLab Solna and online
Suggested number of credits: 0 hp
Enrollment opens: 10 February 2025
Enrollment closes: 30 April 2025
Course fee: No fee
Course lead: Open Science Team (SciLifeLab)
Course contact: training@scilifelab.se

title: Course Syllabus
text:
Course description
This demo course instance showcases how a published course run appears when linked from the landing page as a previous instance.
The course introduces practical Open Science approaches for planning research, sharing data and code responsibly, and communicating results for reuse across Swedish academia.

title: Course Syllabus
text:
Learning Objectives and Learning Outcomes

Learning Objectives

To understand the main Open Science principles used in Swedish research environments
To apply practical actions for planning, sharing, and publishing research outputs



Learning Outcomes
By the end of this course, learners will be able to:

describe the core Open Science concepts used in the course
identify practical steps that make research outputs more open and reusable

title: Course Syllabus
text:
Target audience and course prerequisites
Target audience: doctoral students, postdocs, research support staff Course prerequisites: none Level: beginner/intermediate

title: Course Syllabus
text:
Forms of instruction and examination
Forms of instruction: In-person, digital communications
Forms of study: self-studies (incl. independent work), group activities, lectures, and discussion seminars
Language: English
Examination: Active participation in all sessions and group assignments.

title: Course Syllabus
text:

title: Sharing, publishing, and reuse
text:
Learning Objectives



By the end of this session, learners will be able to:

describe practical routes for sharing data, code, and publications
identify common barriers to reuse and how to reduce them

title: Sharing, publishing, and reuse
text:
Lectures and Materials

FAIR data and responsible sharing practices
Open access publishing and science communication
Enabling reuse through documentation and licensing

title: Open Science foundations
text:
Learning Objectives



By the end of this session, learners will be able to:

explain the core Open Science principles used across the course
connect research planning decisions to openness and reuse

title: Open Science foundations
text:
Lectures and Materials

Introduction to Open Science in the Swedish context
Research planning for openness from the start
Examples of practical actions researchers can adopt immediately
